channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ABS-CBN's Documentary on Flood Management Recognized at International Festival
item.description: ABS-CBN, a leading media and entertainment company in the Philippines, has earned international recognition for its documentary 'Lubog sa Katiwalian', which focuses on flood control and governance iss...

item.title: Congress Protests NEET-UG Paper Leak, Demands Resignation of Education Minister and NTA Dissolution
item.description: On May 21, 2026, hundreds of Congress workers staged a protest near the BJP state headquarters in Jaipur against alleged irregularities in the NEET-UG 2026 examination. The protest, led by state Congr...

item.title: Global Energy Shift: The US Decline and China's Renewable Rise
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing global transition from fossil fuels to renewable energy and the geopolitical consequences of this shift. It highlights how the United States, traditionally dominant i...

item.title: NEWSAN Calls For Stronger Investment And Collaboration To Improve WASH Services In Nigeria
item.description: The Society for Water and Sanitation (NEWSAN) don dey call for better sustainability measures, proper reforms, and make private sector dey more involved to improve Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH)...

item.title: Balancing Anxiety and Aspirations: Facing Life’s Challenges Together
item.description: A 21-year-old writes in, sharing their persistent anxiety about the future, including job prospects, housing, relationships, and broader global concerns like climate change and political instability. ...

item.title: Legal Representative Says Bato Can Be Arrested Without Legal Barriers
item.description: The legal representative of victims of extrajudicial killings (EJKs) has confirmed that there are no legal obstacles preventing the arrest of former police chief Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa. This statemen...

item.title: Senator John Cornyn Faces Public Ridicule for Dairy Queen Campaign Stop in Texas
item.description: Senator John Cornyn of Texas, currently campaigning in a primary runoff, faced widespread mockery after posting about a visit to a Dairy Queen in Sealy, Texas. Cornyn shared a photo with an employee a...

item.title: African Business Leaders Meet in Hong Kong to Explore Partnerships with Asia
item.description: On May 26, 2026, business leaders from various African countries convened in Hong Kong to explore new economic partnerships and strengthen cooperation within the Global South framework. The meeting br...

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns aim for CAF Champions League triumph in Rabat
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns are set to face AS FAR in the second leg of the CAF Champions League final at the Prince Moulay Abdellah Stadium in Rabat on Sunday evening, holding a 1–0 advantage from the first le...

item.title: Tanzanian Civilian Killed in Hormuz Bombing Laid to Rest in Musoma
item.description: A Tanzanian civilian tragically lost their life in a bomb attack that occurred in Hormuz, and the body has been repatriated and buried in Musoma. The funeral gathered local community members who came ...

item.title: Exploring Sisterhood in Literature: How Writers Depict the Bonds Between Sisters
item.description: This essay explores the literary fascination with sisterhood, examining how sisters have been portrayed across novels, fairy tales, and popular fiction. The author begins by reflecting on personal exp...

item.title: Teenager charged after minor league baseball team’s bus destroyed by fire in Winnipeg
item.description: A 15-year-old girl has been charged with arson after the charter bus of the Kane County Cougars, a U.S. minor league baseball team, was deliberately set on fire in Winnipeg. The incident occurred whil...

item.title: Tanzania Blocks European Parliament Delegation, Raising Diplomatic Concerns
item.description: A planned visit by members of the European Parliament to Tanzania was abruptly cancelled after Tanzanian authorities refused to grant permission for the delegation. The visit was supposed to be an off...

item.title: Toni Fowler clarifies his reaction to Alex Gonzaga's pregnancy
item.description: Actress Toni Fowler has shared her reaction to news about fellow celebrity Alex Gonzaga's pregnancy. In a statement, Toni humorously remarked that people might mistakenly think she is the father, clar...

item.title: UCT Students Face Financial Barriers as Rental Costs Outstrip NSFAS Accommodation Allowance
item.description: The University of Cape Town (UCT) Student Representative Council (SRC) has raised alarm over growing financial challenges for students caused by the National Student Financial Aid Scheme's (NSFAS) acc...

item.title: Canadian Armed Forces request return of deployment equipment in Ottawa to address shortages
item.description: The Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) has requested personnel in the Ottawa region who are not currently deployed to return certain deployment equipment, including rucksacks, patrol packs, tactical vests, a...

item.title: Ray Parks Jr. Confirms Receiving Offers from Local and International Basketball Teams
item.description: Filipino basketball star Ray Parks Jr. has kept fans guessing about the next step in his professional career. While he did not disclose exactly where he will be playing next, Parks confirmed that he h...

item.title: Kruger National Park at 100: Conservation, Community and Tourism
item.description: As SANParks marks 100 years of the Kruger National Park, the centenary not only celebrates the park's rich natural heritage but also highlights South Africa's evolving relationship between conservatio...

item.title: PM Modi Emphasises Next-Generation Reforms and Ease of Living in Council of Ministers Meeting
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired a meeting with his Council of Ministers on Thursday in New Delhi, where he laid strong emphasis on implementing next-generation reforms to achieve all-round develo...

item.title: The impact of rude emails on employee wellbeing and workplace dynamics
item.description: Receiving rude or dismissive emails at work is more common than many realise, with research showing that a third of employees encounter at least one per day. Such messages, often termed 'email incivil...
